title: TpmRc pub field exposes raw u32 — no invariant protection or controlled access
description: TpmRc(pub u32) exposes its inner field publicly. The type has an is_success() method suggesting it is meant to be used through its API, but callers can write TpmRc(0) or read .0 directly. If the intent is a value type, the field should be private with an accessor fn raw(&self) -> u32. Public fields on newtypes are idiomatic only when the newtype adds zero semantic meaning — here is_success() proves it adds meaning.
